what_it_does: Creates public.connector_audit, the append-only audit table for the remote MCP gateway. Columns: id (call_id uuid, PK), ts (default now), subject (authed principal/sub claim), scope (e.g. company:design:read|:write), tool, args_preview (truncated args), outcome (OK|DENY|TEACHING-ERROR|DISPATCH-ERROR), detail. Adds three indexes (ts desc, tool, outcome), a table comment, and grants ALL to service_role+postgres and SELECT to anon+authenticated. RLS is deliberately left OFF; access is gated by JWT role.
observations: Every column except args_preview/detail is NOT NULL; id is the call_id rather than a generated surrogate.
outcome is a free-text column with an enumerated convention documented in a comment, not a CHECK constraint or enum type.
Header comment states audit-write failure must make the call fail loud (mandatory-audit guard, attributed to a 'lead ruling').
RLS intentionally disabled with rationale: append-only system log, access gated by JWT role not row policies.
Grants SELECT to both anon and authenticated for the operator UI.
standouts: The mandatory-audit-or-fail-loud design intent recorded in the header comment.
Logs the four outcome categories including TEACHING-ERROR and DISPATCH-ERROR as first-class results.
conventions: Uses create table/index if not exists for idempotent migration replay.
Documents the schema and policy rationale inline via comments — the repo's self-description discipline.
concerns: outcome and scope are unconstrained text; a typo'd value would be silently accepted (no enum/CHECK).
anon SELECT on an audit log exposes principal/tool/scope data to the public anon key (intentional per comment, but broad).
notes: The mandatory audit log for the remote MCP gateway — one row per remote tool call, RLS-off, JWT-role-gated, write-failure-is-fatal.
questions: Where is the fail-loud-on-audit-write-failure guard enforced (the table itself does not enforce it)?
Why expose audit rows to anon rather than only authenticated?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: header states it is the mandatory audit table; the DDL creates exactly that with the documented columns and grants.
apparent_themes: mandatory accountability / non-repudiation of remote calls
role-in-JWT access control over row-level policy
intention_signals: Make every remote MCP tool call leave an unavoidable, queryable trace (who/scope/tool/outcome) so the gateway can refuse to act when it cannot record what it did.
novelty: Treating audit-write success as a precondition for the call succeeding, and the explicit choice to skip RLS in favor of JWT-role gating for a system log.
contribution: Brings the auditing substrate for the remote connector — the schema and grant posture for recording every gateway call with its principal, scope, tool, and outcome.
summary_for_embedding: SQL migration creating the connector_audit append-only table (call_id/ts/subject/scope/tool/outcome) with indexes and service_role+read grants, RLS off, for the remote MCP gateway.
intention_for_embedding: To guarantee every remote MCP gateway tool call is recorded with its principal, scope, tool, and outcome so the gateway can fail loud when it cannot audit.
